parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ok: true, tier: $('Build Log Row').first().json.tier, score: $('Build Log Row').first().json.score }) }}

parameters.text: =:warning: Lead pipeline step failed for {{ $json.leadEmail }} at {{ $json.at }}. Check executions.

parameters.content:
## Security + Validation Layer
Webhook receives the lead, then the Code node:
1. Verifies an HMAC-SHA256 signature (X-Signature header) using a secret from $env, with a constant-time compare.
2. Validates required fields and email format.
3. Sanitizes input and caps lengths (also limits prompt-injection surface).
Unsigned or invalid requests are rejected with a clean 4xx before any LLM call.

## AI Scoring with Guardrails
Claude is called via HTTP (header-auth credential, no key in the node). Retries 3x with backoff. User text is fenced in <lead> tags and treated as data, not instructions. The Parse & Guard node never trusts the model blindly: it parses defensively, validates the JSON shape, clamps the score, and falls back to manual review. If the API fails entirely, the error-output branch still routes the lead to review.

## Routing, Logging, Failover
Hot leads trigger a Slack alert + a personalized auto-reply. Warm/cold are logged quietly. Every lead is written to a Google Sheets audit log (no secrets logged). Slack/Email/Sheets each have an error output that funnels into a single failure alert, so a transient API issue never silently drops a lead.
